title: China warns of alleged Claude Code backdoor risk; Anthropic calls it anti-abuse
friendlyTitle: Claude Code faces a security warning in China
summary: Channel News Asia reports that China’s National Vulnerability Database warned of a “security backdoor” risk in versions of Anthropic’s Claude Code, alleging it could transmit sensitive information to Anthropic servers without consent. Anthropic described the mechanism as an anti-abuse measure tied to prohibited access from restricted regions.
friendlySummary: Chinese authorities warned about a possible Claude Code security risk, while Anthropic says the feature is meant to prevent misuse.
whyItMatters: AI coding agents are powerful because they sit close to source code, terminals, and developer machines. The dispute is a reminder that enterprises should inspect telemetry, policy controls, and regional compliance before approving coding agents broadly.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Companies using AI coding tools should understand what data the tools send and what controls are available.
